briefdescription.para: prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros.
Maximum allowed re-checking locality, prescribed by LLC.
Maximum allowed re-checking content stream management.
Unique receiver Id size in bytes.
Max number of downstream devices allowed.
AKE No Stored Km message size.
Repeater Auth send receiver ID list message.
Repeater Auth send receiver ID list maximum message size.
RepeaterAuth send ack message size in bytes.
RepeaterAuth stream manage message size in bytes.
RepeaterAuth stream ready message size in bytes.
Reason why the timer was started: Undefined.
Reason why the timer was started: Waiting for Content Stream Type to be set when in repeater mode.

simplesect.para: : The message ids also double as a reason identifier. Thus, the value of this define should NOT overlap a message Id.

briefdescription.para: Reason why the timer was started: Mandatory wait of 200 ms before the cipher may be activated.

detaileddescription.para:
Authenticated flag is only set after this period has expired.
: The message ids also double as a reason identifier. Thus, the value of this define should NOT overlap a message Id.

briefdescription.para: Reason why the timer was started: Status checking.

simplesect.para: : The message ids also double as a reason identifier. Thus, the value of this define should NOT overlap a message Id.

briefdescription.para: Internal used timer counter for timeout checking.
Internal used timer counter for logging.
HDCP Port DDC Rx status register masks.
RX status available bytes in read message buffer.
RX certificate and Tx public key sizes in bytes.

detaileddescription.para: Unique receiver Id size in the RX certificate.

briefdescription.para: Public key-N size in the RX certificate.
Public key-E size in the RX certificate.
Reserved size in the RX certificate.
Signature size in the RX certificate.
Total size of the RX certificate.
Receiver Id size in the SRM block.
Signature size in the SRM block.
Stream ID and Type size in bytes.
Use a pairing info Ekh(Km) test vector, use i.c.w XHDCP22_TX_TEST_RCV_TIMEOUT.
AKE is forced using a stored Km scenario.

detaileddescription.para: Pairing info is pre-loaded with test vectors that forces Stored Km scenario.
this is mainly used to check the HDCP RX core in loopback mode in case it cannot meet timing requirements (no offloading to hardware) and reponsetimes need to be logged.

briefdescription.para: Pairing info is cleared, to force a non-stored Km scenario.
Actual HDCP2.2 RX component is connected.
HDCP2.2 RX software component is not available and will be emulated.
HDCP2.2 RX is emulated, XHdcp22Tx_LogDisplay shows source code.
Use test keys as defined in Errata to HDCP on HDMI Specification Revision 2.2, February 09, 2015.
Last value the list, only used for checking.

detaileddescription.para: This enumeration defines the various test modes available for the HDCP 2.2 transmitter driver. These modes are used for testing and validation purposes during development and integration.

simplesect.para: The test modes should be used carefully and are primarily intended for development and debugging scenarios.

detaileddescription.para: These values are used as parameter for the XHDCP22_TX_LOG_EVT_DBG logging event.

memberdef.argsstring: (u8 *Output, const u8 *InputA, const u8 *InputB, unsigned int Size)
memberdef.name: XHdcp22Tx_MemXor

briefdescription.para: This function calculates a XOR on a array.

parameterdescription.para: is the XOR of the input arrays.
is a input array.
is a input array.
is the size of the buffers to XOR.

memberdef.argsstring: (const XHdcp22_Tx_CertRx *CertificatePtr, const u8 *KpubDcpNPtr, int KpubDcpNSize, const u8 *KpubDcpEPtr, int KpubDcpESize)
memberdef.name: XHdcp22Tx_VerifyCertificate

briefdescription.para: This function verifies the HDCP receivers certificate.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the certificate from the HDCP2.2 receiver.
is a pointer to the N-value of the certificate signature.
is the size of the N-value of the certificates signature.
iis a pointer to the E-value of the certificate signature.
is the size of the E-value of the certificates signature.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the certificate is valid.
XST_FAILURE if the certificate is invalid.

memberdef.argsstring: (const u8 *SrmPtr, int SrmSize, const u8 *KpubDcpNPtr, int KpubDcpNSize, const u8 *KpubDcpEPtr, int KpubDcpESize)
memberdef.name: XHdcp22Tx_VerifySRM

briefdescription.para: This function verifies a HDCP2 system renewability message (SRM) block.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the SRM.
is the size of the SRM in bytes.
is a pointer to the N-value of the DCP LLC key.
is the size of the N-value of the DCP LLC key.
iis a pointer to the E-value of the DCP LLC key.
is the size of the E-value of the DCP LLC key.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the SRM block is valid.
XST_FAILURE if the SRM block is invalid.

memberdef.argsstring: (const u8 *Rrx, const u8 *RxCaps, const u8 *Rtx, const u8 *TxCaps, const u8 *Km, u8 *HPrime)
memberdef.name: XHdcp22Tx_ComputeHPrime

parameterdescription.para: is the Rx random generated value on start of authentication.
are the capabilities of the receiver.
is the Tx random generated value start of authentication.
are the capabilities of the receiver.
is the master key generated by tx.
is a pointer to the HPrime hash from the HDCP2.2 receiver.

memberdef.argsstring: (const u8 *Rn, const u8 *Km, const u8 *Rrx, const u8 *Rtx, u8 *LPrime)
memberdef.name: XHdcp22Tx_ComputeLPrime

detaileddescription.para: Kd is calculated as in XHdcp22Tx_ComputeHPrime, but could also be stored the first time. This could save some computing time. According protocol however, only Rrx and Rtx are supposed to be stored.

parameterdescription.para: is a pseudo-random nonce.
is the master key generated by tx and previouslyd stored.
is the random value generated by rx.
is the random value generated by tx.
is a pointer to the computed LPrime hash.

memberdef.argsstring: (const u8 *Rn, const u8 *Rrx, const u8 *RxInfo, const u8 *Rtx, const u8 *RecvIDList, const u8 RecvIDCount, const u8 *SeqNum_V, const u8 *Km, u8 *V)
memberdef.name: XHdcp22Tx_ComputeV

memberdef.argsstring: (const u8 *Rn, const u8 *Rrx, const u8 *Rtx, const u8 *StreamIDType, const u8 *k, const u8 *SeqNum_M, const u8 *Km, u8 *M)
memberdef.name: XHdcp22Tx_ComputeM

briefdescription.para: Computes the M hash value for HDCP 2.2 authentication.

detaileddescription.para: This function calculates the M parameter used in the HDCP 2.2 protocol by processing the input parameters through a cryptographic hash function.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the Rn random number.
Pointer to the Rrx receiver random number.
Pointer to the Rtx transmitter random number.
Pointer to the Stream ID Type value.
Pointer to the k parameter.
Pointer to the sequence number for M.
Pointer to the Km master key.
Pointer to the output buffer where computed M hash will be stored..

simplesect.para: The caller must ensure all input pointers are valid and point to buffers of appropriate size.
The output buffer M must be pre-allocated with sufficient size to store the computed hash value.

memberdef.argsstring: (const u8 *Rn, const u8 *Km, const u8 *Ks, const u8 *Rrx, const u8 *Rtx, u8 *EdkeyKs)
memberdef.name: XHdcp22Tx_ComputeEdkeyKs

detaileddescription.para: Kd is calculated as in XHdcp22Tx_ComputeHPrime, but could also be stored the first time. This could save some computing time. According protocol however, only Rrx and Rtx are supposed to be stored.

parameterdescription.para: is a pseudo-random nonce.
is the master key generated by tx.. See also XHdcp22Tx_ComputeHPrime
is the session key.
is the random value generated by rx.
is the random value generated by tx.
is the encrypted Ks output.

briefdescription.para: This function encrypts the Km value with the receivers public key into Ekh(Km)

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the certificate from the HDCP2.2 receiver.
is the random master key.
is a pointer to a 32 byte seed for masking in the MGF1 function used in RSA-OEAP encryption.
output of the encryption with a 128 byte (1024 bit) size.

detaileddescription.para:
Otherwise it uses offloading from hardware.
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XHdcp22_Tx core instance.
NumOctets
is the number of octets in the random number.
RandomNumberPtr
is a pointer to the random number.
None.
